you_are_here: Phases 4, 5, 7 active — Rubric · Sourcing Layer · Data Capture. Engagement re-engaged: 9 July working session held, 50-search PoC agreed and underway. Matching engine (Phase 6) held pending PoC data.

summary.build_time: Sourcing Assistant v2 live and restored. 50-search PoC is the unlock path for the matching engine.

naming_note.17_Ishwa_Apps_Script.js: Bound to Google Sheet — deployed, same URL
naming_note.18_Ishwa_Mandate_Reference.xlsx: Shared with Somanshu — internally tracked as 17 on Drive/Sheets

phases.name: Scoping & Diagnosis
phases.title: Engagement scoping & problem diagnosis
phases.desc: Structured strategy session with Shalini Gupta. Problem diagnosed: Ishwa's quality edge lives entirely in consultant judgement — no documented framework, no structured AI input. Solution architecture defined across three layers.

deliverables.label: Problem framing & solution architecture
Engagement folder delivered to client

phases.name: Data Audit
phases.title: Data receipt, audit & documentation
phases.desc: Five data files received from Ishwa (Zoho Recruit + CRM, covering Sep 2022 — Mar 2026). Full audit completed. Three structural gaps identified. Audit documents, data capture templates, interactive forms, and the ChatGPT Search Assistant built and delivered.

deliverables.label: 01 — Leadership data audit
02 — Consultant data audit
03 — Data gap one-pager
04/05 — Intake & closure forms (superseded by 15)
06 — Project tracker (live)
07 — ChatGPT Search Assistant
08/09 — Meeting one-pager & agenda

milestone.label: All audit documents, templates and meeting prep delivered

phases.name: Client Inputs
phases.title: 8 April session — decisions locked, build unblocked
phases.desc: Three-step workflow agreed. Data capture path confirmed (Option C — web forms). Order book received from Somanshu and analysed. Meeting minutes, order book analysis, and rubric scaffold sent to Ishwa on 22 April. Both forms live with Ishwa as of 24 April.

deliverables.label: 11 — Meeting minutes (8 Apr)
12A/12B — Order book analysis (client + internal)
13A — Rubric scaffold — sent to Ishwa
13B — Walkthrough guide — sent to overseer
15 — Combined mandate data entry form — supersedes 04 and 05
Apps Script (Google Sheets integration) — v3 active

milestone.label: Forms live with Ishwa. Data flowing to Google Sheets.

phases.name: Rubric Build
phases.title: Rubric calibration — now advancing through the master sheet
phases.desc: Rubric substance (weightages, must-haves, dealbreakers, compensation philosophy) advancing through master-sheet validation — agreed path from the 9 July session. 13A document review superseded by this path (13 Jul decision): same data points captured, validated by client-side team. Rubric feeds the matching engine in Phase 6 and the profile scoring logic in Phase 5.

deliverables.label: 13A rubric scaffold — superseded by master-sheet validation path
Weightages + dealbreakers validated via master sheet
Rubric walkthrough session (Varsha as participant)
Somanshu screenshare — data repositories

milestone.label: Weightages and dealbreakers validated in master sheet — rubric v1 equivalent

phases.name: Sourcing Layer
phases.title: Sourcing compression — primary problem being addressed
phases.desc: AI Sourcing Assistant v2 live as of 14 May 2026; tool maintenance 13 July (updated to current AI model, output layout hardened for long generated labels). Major upgrade from v1: multi-title role input, multi-select seniority, Company Stage optional, Key Skills field, expanded sectors, Education field, up to 50 companies per output (mid-tier weighted), Resdex strings appear first, primary + secondary string, company count badge per segment, Copy All and Download XLSX export. 9 July: no organic adoption of the tool to date — verified the link has not been accessed. This is a distribution question, not a tool-quality verdict. Comparison session (Sourcing Assistant vs ChatGPT) to be scheduled for week of 13 July — effectively the tool's first structured exposure to the team. Sourcing tool does not touch Naukri — it replaces the pre-Naukri ChatGPT step only.

deliverables.label: 14 — Sourcing workflow analysis — shared with Ishwa (live screenshare with Somanshu)
16 — AI Sourcing Assistant v2 — live on GitHub Pages
Vercel proxy deployed — API routed through PiP_AGI
Screen recordings received and analysed (Aparna)
Feedback from consultants incorporated in v2
Tool maintenance — current AI model + output layout hardening (13 Jul)
Comparison session — Sourcing Assistant vs ChatGPT (Amit to set up with Shalini + Somanshu, week of 13 Jul)
Model selection moved to Vercel proxy — maintenance hardening
LinkedIn Recruiter status confirmed with Shalini
200 → 10 scoring design — soft ask, not a blocker

milestone.label: Sourcing assistant v2 live. Comparison session is the adoption gateway.

phases.name: Matching Engine
phases.title: Matching engine prototype — deliberately held pending form data
phases.desc: AI evaluation layer. Takes structured mandate JSON + candidate profiles, returns ranked shortlist with per-criterion scores and plain-language rationale. Architecture designed. Build deliberately held — will be calibrated against mandate form data (Section 5 notes: named candidates, accept/reject reasoning, CVs). 3 mandates and 11 candidates available as seed data from Varsha (May 2026). Build resumes once meaningful form dataset exists (target: 50+ entries with notes).

deliverables.label: System prompt architecture
Seed data received — 3 mandates, 11 candidates, verdicts from Varsha
API prototype built
Tested against closed mandates
Ishwa review and sign-off

phases.blocker: Deliberately held. The 50-search PoC (agreed 9 Jul, target completion ~end July) is the unlock path — build resumes once PoC entries land with Section 5 notes populated.

phases.name: Data Capture
phases.title: Data capture — 50-search proof of concept agreed 9 July
phases.desc: 9 July working session restructured the approach: instead of distributing all ~400 searches at once (client-side data-quality concern), a proof of concept with 50 recent and significant searches (excluding DMACC), prioritising clients where the team works directly with founders or senior leadership. Client-level data collected first (~135 clients — context, reason, stage, sector, compensation); working with whatever the client provides. Soft search strategy section removed from the schema (difficult to implement). 300–400 pre-populated line items to be validated by Somanshu's team, weightages added to master sheet. Section 5 depth (named candidates, accept/reject reasoning, CVs) remains the gold standard per Varsha's format.

deliverables.label: 17 — Queue form — live on GitHub Pages
17 — Apps Script v3 — bound to Google Sheet, deployed
18 — Mandate Reference Sheet — 409 mandates, 60 columns, sent to Somanshu
Intake_Log + Closure_Log tabs — auto-created on first submission
Queue form added to engagement hub (13 Jul) — was live but not linked
9 Jul — 50-search PoC agreed (ex-DMACC, founder-led clients prioritised)
Soft search strategy section removed from schema (9 Jul)
Somanshu — 50-search list by Thu 16 Jul, criteria shared for client approval
Client-level data for 135 clients to Amit — due Mon 13 Jul
Team entry — 10 in-depth searches each (Chhaya, Namrata, Varsha, Mohit + Shalini, to confirm) — target end of next week
Daily review cadence from Mon 13 Jul
Section 5 notes with candidate reasoning + CVs — CRITICAL PATH

milestone.label: 50-search PoC completed with Section 5 depth — unlocks matching engine build.

phases.name: BD Analytics
phases.title: BD pattern analysis — strategic intelligence layer
phases.desc: Pattern analysis across 3+ years of mandate data. Close rates by sector, average search duration by mandate type, ghost client signals, BD pipeline conversion. Delivered as a strategic insight report for leadership.

deliverables.label: Industry label standardisation
BD pattern report
Mandate risk scoring model

milestone.label: BD analytics report delivered to Ishwa leadership

open_actions.note: No organic access to the tool to date — treat the session as first structured exposure, not a re-trial.

open_actions.action: Confirm receipt — client-level data for 135 clients (due from Somanshu Mon 13 Jul)
open_actions.note: Client context, reason, stage, sector, compensation. Work with whatever arrives.

open_actions.action: Review Somanshu's 50-search list + criteria (due Thu 16 Jul)
open_actions.note: Ex-DMACC, founder/senior-leadership clients. Criteria shared for client approval.

open_actions.action: Track PoC entry cadence — daily review from 13 Jul, 10 per person, target end of next week
open_actions.note: Section 5 depth is the point — named candidates, accept/reject reasoning, CVs.

open_actions.action: Confirm fifth PoC entrant (transcript said 'Ishwa' — assumed Shalini)
open_actions.note: Somanshu holds the distributor role; confirm before workload is assumed.

open_actions.action: Move model selection from Sourcing Assistant page into Vercel proxy
open_actions.note: Single-edit maintenance for all tools on any future model change; keeps internal model names out of client-facing errors.

open_actions.action: Recurring check — vendor AI model deprecation notices
LinkedIn Recruiter status confirmation with Ishwa
Soft ask — Resdex profile review recording

key_decisions.decision: Engagement re-engaged — 9 Jul working session held
key_decisions.rationale: Resolves the 29 June chase. Session with Shalini and Somanshu produced a concrete data plan with owners and dates.
Client concern: full-set distribution to the broader team risks inaccurate entries. 50 recent, significant searches (ex-DMACC), founder-led clients, 10 per person, daily review.
Less complex to gather than search-specific detail; unblocks analysis while search-level entry proceeds. Working with whatever the client provides.

key_decisions.decision: Soft search strategy section removed from schema
key_decisions.rationale: Agreed 9 Jul — difficult to implement in practice.

Same data points captured (weightages, must-haves, dealbreakers, comp philosophy), validated by the client-side team — stronger endorsement than a document review. Clears the 13A blocker open since April.

key_decisions.decision: Model selection to move into Vercel proxy
key_decisions.rationale: Pinned model versions in the page require per-tool edits on vendor model changes. Proxy-side selection makes any future change a single edit covering all tools.
Re-work risk too high with 11 candidate samples. Will build once 50+ mandate form entries with Section 5 notes exist. Forms are primary training data — not recordings.

key_decisions.decision: Varsha verdict format is the gold standard for Section 5
key_decisions.rationale: Named candidates, brief reasoning per candidate, CVs attached — this is exactly the signal the matching engine needs. Communicated to Somanshu in 14 May email.
